observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to:
a.1 Prepare and maintain all the required property reports and records, such
as the Report on Local Road Networks, Local Road Inventory and Road
Map, and Local Road Network Property Cards;
a.2 Ensure that inventory reports are independently prepared based on actual
physical count results and contain complete and essential information;
a.3 Periodically reconcile property records with accounting records to ensure
the proper identification and correction of any discrepancies; and
a.4 Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) to account for all LGU properties that are subject to disposal;
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
b.1 Reassess and recompute the adjustments made to the Road Networks
account and properly recognize the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation
for the newly recorded road assets contained in the Inventory Report of the
Municipal Engineer; and
b.2 Update the ledger cards for road networks and ensure that all pertinent
details are complete and consistent with the format prescribed in Annex D of
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015;
c. The Municipal Engineer to provide the basis, together with supporting
documents, for the valuation of the cost of concrete reflected in the Road
Inventory furnished to the Municipal Accountant for the necessary
adjustment, as appropriate;
d. Perform the One-Time Cleansing of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No.
2020-006 to be able to establish PPE Balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability; and
e. Direct the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance Revised
Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties prescribed in COA-DBM
Joint Circular No. 2024-1, dated January 30, 2024 to ensure proper
derecognition of these assets from the books, leading to a more accurate PPE
valuation in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management strictly require the following:
a. The Inventory Committee to:
a.1 Conduct a complete semestral physical count of inventories;
a.2 Ensure that the required information in the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) is complete; and
a.3 Comply with the submission of the RPCI on or before July 31 and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to perform periodic and
complete reconciliation of inventory records;
c. The Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method in
recording the purchases of supplies and materials;
d. The Municipal Treasurer to strictly implement the proper procedures in the
requisition and issuance of supplies and materials; and
e. The end-users or concerned departments to specifically indicate the required
delivery schedule from suppliers in the Purchase Request and other relevant
procurement documents to address the absence of adequate storage facilities
within the Municipality.

We recommended that Management direct the following:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to:
a.1 Include only released checks in the monthly Report of Check Issued (RCI)
for submission to the Accounting Office; and
a.2 Ensure that the actual date of receipt of payments is reflected in the
disbursement vouchers (DVs) and official receipts (ORs); and
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
b.1 Record only transactions in the RCI which are supported with a duly
issued Official Receipt (ORs) from suppliers or properly acknowledged in the
DVs by the payees; and
b.2 Record unissued checks as payables instead of disbursements until such
time they are released to the payee.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Auditor the
lacking documentation, supportive calculations, bank records, and other items necessary
to substantiate the accuracy and appropriateness of the credit adjustment made to the
Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account, amounting ₱858,258.95.

We recommended that Management direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
a.1. Determine the accurate year-end amount of the unexpended balance of
the 1st Tranche of the Philippine Multi-Sectoral Nutrition Program
(PMNP) Performance-Based Grant (PBG) transferred by the DOH-CHD
Field Office No. V in CY 2024;
a.2. Based on the amount arrived at in the preceding step, prepare adjusting
journal entries necessary to record the amount under the appropriate
liability and equity accounts as prescribed in Chapter 10 of the PMNP
Project Operations Manual; and
a.3. Recognize the receipt of the next tranches of PBG, if any, as an
outright income, and the subsequent utilization thereof under appropriate
asset and expense accounts in compliance with the provisions of the
Operations Manual; and
b. Ensure that the required financial reports under the Tripartite Memorandum
of Agreement and the Operations Manual, including the monthly Municipal
Statement of Receipt and Expenditure (SORD) and Municipal Certificate of
Status of Funds (CSF), are timely prepared by the Municipal Technical
Manager and Municipal Accounting Office, reviewed by the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, and endorsed by the Municipal Health
Officer and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, as co-chairs
of the PMNP-Municipal Technical Working Group (MTWG), for approval
by the local chief executive, and submitted to the concerned CHD.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to ensure that Obligation
Requests (OBRs) are completely and accurately filled out with all required
information, including particulars and account codes, to serve as a proper
reference for the accurate recording of disbursements and budget utilization;
and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that only expenses not covered
by the regular budget allocation, but incurred by the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) in the performance of official functions, and properly charged under
the appropriation for discretionary funds, are recorded under the
Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) account.

We recommended that Management:
a. Demand the immediate liquidation of overdue cash advances and cause
the withholding of the payment of salary of the AOs if no liquidation is
received after 30 days from demand;
b. Send demand letters to persons previously connected with the LGU who
still have unliquidated cash advances, and furnish the Audit Team of the
duly received demand letters, otherwise, hold the officials responsible for
clearing said individuals from money accountability despite unsettlement
of granted cash advances;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the grant of cash advances
and enforce strictly the immediate liquidation thereof;
d. Ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or
employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a
proper accounting thereof is made; and
e. File appropriate legal action, if warranted, against those who were unable
to liquidate the overdue CAs after exhausting appropriate means
including those who are no longer in government service.

We recommended that Management:
a. Provide, if possible, a secured storage room for the supplies and materials
of the LGU;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
b.1 Strictly implement the proper procedures in the requisition and
issuance of supplies and materials; and
b.2 Maintain complete documentation for supplies and materials, such as
stock cards, inspection and acceptance reports, requisition issue slips
(RIS), and summary of supplies and materials issued (SSMI);
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
c.1 Adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the purchases of
supplies and materials; and
c.2 Apply the weighted average method of costing the inventory items;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer of the
periodic reconciliation of inventory records; and
e. Require the Inventory Committee to:
e.1 Conduct a complete semestral physical count of inventories;
e.2 Ensure that the required information in the Report on Physical Count
of Inventories (RPCI) is complete; and
e.3 Comply with the submission of the RPCI on or before July 31 and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


       a. implement the procedures prescribed under  COA  Circular  2020-006
          dated January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical  count  of  PPE,
          recognition of PPE items found at station and disposition for non-
          existing/missing PPE items  for  the  one-time  cleansing  of  PPE
          account balances of government  agencies  to  produce  a  reliable
          report and PPE balances;


       b.  require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  prepare  the   necessary
          adjustments in the books to take  up  the  completed  projects  in
          order to come up  with  the  accurate  balances  of  the  CIP  and
          appropriate asset accounts;


       c. direct the Municipal Accountant to record all  road  networks  and
          its components, based on the Registry of  Public  Infrastructures,
          or in its absence, based on  the  complete  Local  Road  Inventory
          Report provided with complete description and cost segregation  of
          the road components and provide  proper  disclosure  of  the  road
          networks in the Notes to Financial Statements;


       d. direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated  Local  Road
          Inventory and Road Map and keep  a  complete  Local  Road  Network
          Property Card of all roads within its area of jurisdiction;


       e. require the Inventory Committee  to  conduct  inventory  of  local
          roads, highways and bridges within its area of jurisdiction  which
          were acquired thru purchase, construction or donations;


       f. require the  Municipal  Accountant  to  provide  for  depreciation
          allowances for Infrastructure Assets  and  prepare  the  necessary
          adjusting entries in the government equity  for  the  unrecognized
          depreciation expense;


       g. require coordination  and  reconciliation  of  records  among  the
          Offices of  the  Municipal  Accountant,  Municipal  Treasurer  and
          Municipal Assessor for the accurate recording of  real  properties
          owned by the LGU; and


       h.  require  the  Municipal  Assessor’s  Office  to  facilitate   the
          registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of title of  the
          aforementioned parcels of lots in the name of  the  LGU  with  the
          Registry of Deeds.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:


       a. reconcile the balance of CIB-LCCA account per books and per bank
          and effect the necessary adjustments;


       b. coordinate with the PVB to secure the necessary  documents  needed
          in the booking up/recording of the DSRA fund under CIB-LCSA; and


       c. record appropriate entries in the books to recognize all the debit
          and credit memos reflected in the passbook of the savings account.

We recommended that Management require the:

       a. Municipal Treasurer to perform  the  functions  of  receiving  and
          issuance of delivered items as prescribed in the NGAS  Manual  for
          LGUs;


       b. Inventory Committee to  diligently  conduct  semestral  counts  of
          supplies and materials,  and  to  ensure  timely  preparation  and
          submission of the Report of the Physical Count of  Inventories  to
          the Audit Team;

       c. Municipal Treasurer to religiously prepare  the  SSMI  to  provide
          basis for the recording of inventory issuances  by  the  Municipal
          Accountant;

       d. Municipal Accountant to implement Perpetual Inventory Method  when
          recording purchases of inventory for stock as  prescribed  in  the
          Manual; and


       e. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  Stock
          Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively,  and  to  reconcile
          the same periodically.

We recommended that Management:

       a. discontinue acting as a  collecting  agent  of  the  banks  and/or
          private lending institutions and using government fund to pay  for
          the loans of the concerned personnel in case  of  default  of  the
          latter;


       b. desist from accommodating LGU personnel to obtain a new loan  when
          it is already beyond their capacity to pay; and


       c. formulate policy and sound internal control on loan application of
          LGU personnel.

      We also recommended the Municipal Accountant:


       a. maintain  subsidiary  ledgers  for  each  personnel  per  loan  or
          borrowing;


       b. reconcile the discrepancies noted and prepare an adjusting  entry,
          if necessary; and


       c. present the journal entry vouchers for the posted  collections  of
          payments/remittances for loan repayments in March,  2021  totaling
          ₱56,787.31 and its corresponding supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: a.) allow only cash advances for salaries and wages, commutable allowances, honoraria and other similar payments for maintenance and operating expenses which cannot be paid conveniently by check or are required to be paid immediately pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and b.) ensure that elected officials may be granted a cash advance only for their official travelling expenses.
We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: c.) record goods for distribution to Welfare Goods for Distribution account and record the same upon distribution to Welfare Good Expense account; and e.) submit the distributions lists on distributed relief items to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary update on the RPTAR to reflect the true and correct amount of RPT and SET Receivables and regularly monitor the same and submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated and reliable list of RPT and SET receivables for all real property owners of the LGU: and
Municipal Accountant to effect adequate recording of Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts and provide and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the accounts.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory taking of supplies and materials once every semester and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Audit Team within the set timeline;
require the concerned offices in-charge to submit to the Municipal Accountant the required SSMI, together with the original copies of the RIS as basis in the issuance of supplies and recording of the issued/distributed supplies; and
require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock Cards and Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each type of Inventory.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) create a committee composed of one personnel each from General Services and Accounting Office who shall reconcile their respective records of PPE on a full time basis and at the same time maintain updated and complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; and
b) in ensuing years, ensure that the conduct of physical count of PPE is in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, prescribing the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record:
a.) 	to the appropriate PPE account in the General Fund books after completion of the projects under the Trust Fund and accordingly record depreciation;
b.) to the appropriate CIP account all on-going projects during construction period as provided under Section 4 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
c.) the necessary adjusting entries on the unrecorded depreciation expense   on completed projects.
We further recommended that Management require the contractor to replace the blank official/collection receipts issued on the payment of infrastructure projects to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize costs incurred for the capital improvements of existing assets and procurement of office equipment and recognize its corresponding depreciation and accordingly issue Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PAR) to the employees accountable to the properties.

We recommended that Management create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible for the immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations to avoid its further deterioration.

We recommended and Management agreed to in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan issue policy guidelines on the disposition and payment of communication and internet allowances and consider the use of mobile phone fixed plan offered by telecommunication networks.
We further recommended and Management agreed to require:
the submission of the support documentation for the payment made to Smart Communications, Inc. amounting to ₱15,000.00 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension; and
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims for communication allowances was supported with certification signed by the agency head or his duly authorized representative that the amounts claimed are used for official purposes only coupled with the Monthly Statement of Accounts/ used cell cards other than the proof of payments to substantiate consumption or usage.

We recommended that Management ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from the monthly honoraria paid to its consultant and national government agency officials/employees assigned in the LGU and remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR).

We recommended and Management agreed to strategically plan and implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and to undertake critical, urgent, and appropriate disaster response aid and measures to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19, as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2017-1 and 01.

We recommended that Management ensure that only those conventions, trainings and seminars which are highly relevant, mandatory and responsive to the capacity-building needs or requirements of the LGU are attended, prioritized and participated in by the most qualified people in the LGU to avoid any wasteful and inefficient utilization of fund.
